steps.text:
No, that line is not entirely accurate to publish as-is and needs to be clarified. 

According to Acme's official pricing page, the actual monthly subscription rate is **$39/month** if billed month-to-month. 

The **$29/month** figure cited by your comparison articles and review sites is only available as an **annual plan** ($348 billed upfront for the year). 

To ensure your market brief is accurate and doesn't mislead readers, you should revise the line. Here are a few ways you can write it:

* **Option 1 (Detailed):** *"Acme's Standard plan costs $39/month, or $29/month if billed annually ($348/year)."*
* **Option 2 (Strictly monthly):** *"Acme's month-to-month subscription costs $39/month."*
* **Option 3 (Clarifying the discount):** *"Acme charges $29/month, billed annually."*

* **Actual Burn Rate:** $\$840,000$ spent over $14$ months is **$\$60,000/\text{month}$** (not $\$70,000$).

* **Actual Runway:** With $\$700,000$ remaining and a true burn rate of $\$60,000/\text{month}$, you have **$11.7\text{ months}$** of runway (not $10$ months).
  *(Calculation: $\$700,000 \div \$60,000 = 11.67\text{ months}$)*

This is actually great news for your pitch. Your burn rate is lower and your runway is longer than you thought. Presenting a $\$60\text{k}$ burn rate with nearly $12$ months of runway looks much better to investors than $\$70\text{k}$ and $10$ months.
